meta_title: For Students: marketing automation to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to marketing automation and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Marketing Automation to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to use marketing automation to overcome the 'no project ideas' block. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Overcome the 'no project ideas' block by building a marketing automation tool that solves a real pain point. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative development, you create a high-impact portfolio piece that demonstrates both technical proficiency and an understanding of business-critical automation workflows.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the developer’s greatest adversary. You have the skills, you have the hunger, but you’re paralyzed by the 'what do I build?' dilemma. You don’t need another generic To-Do app; you need a project that solves a real-world problem for businesses—marketing automation.

By automating lead outreach or social media scheduling, you shift from being a coder to a solution architect. This case study will guide you through building a functional, high-value marketing tool, transforming your portfolio from academic exercises to tangible business assets.

steps.title: Define the Automation Logic
steps.description: Before writing code, map out the workflow. We are building a script that pulls data from a Google Sheet and sends personalized emails via an API.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior software architect. Design a modular architecture for a marketing automation tool that reads lead data from a CSV and sends personalized outreach emails using the Resend API. Provide a folder structure and a list of necessary dependencies.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create the project directory structure and initialize a package.json with the dependencies defined in the architecture plan.'

steps.title: Build the Data Ingestion Module
steps.description: Create the engine that parses your marketing leads. This teaches you how to handle data sanitization, a critical skill for any developer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust Node.js module that reads a CSV file, validates email formats, and returns a clean array of JSON objects for the automation engine.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a csvParser.js file that implements the validation logic and includes basic error handling for malformed rows.'

steps.title: Integrate the Email Service
steps.description: Connect your application to an external API. This is where your code begins to interact with the outside world.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a service function for the Resend API that accepts an email object and sends a personalized template string. Include logging for success and failure states.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Implement emailService.js and ensure all API keys are handled via environment variables using dotenv.'

steps.title: Implement the Orchestration Loop
steps.description: This is the 'brain' of your automation. It coordinates the data ingestion and the email dispatching.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an orchestration script that loops through the parsed lead data, calls the email service for each entry, and implements a rate-limiting delay to prevent API throttling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create main.js to import the parser and service modules, then implement the main execution loop with a 2-second delay between requests.'

steps.title: Testing and Deployment
steps.description: Verify your code works and prepare it for the world. We will use mock data to ensure safety before live execution.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a test suite using Jest to verify that the CSV parser handles empty files and the email service correctly catches API errors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'Run the test suite and fix any identified edge cases in the data validation logic.'

pro_tips: Always use environment variables for API keys; never hardcode credentials, even in local development.
Implement exponential backoff in your API loops to prevent your app from crashing during heavy traffic.
Add a 'dry-run' mode that logs actions to the console instead of triggering real API calls during testing.
Focus on modularity; separate your data logic from your transport logic so you can swap email providers later.
Document your API responses in a README file; this shows future employers you understand production-level maintenance.
content.next_steps: Once the script is running, deploy it to a serverless function (like AWS Lambda or Vercel) and add a simple dashboard to track your automation logs.
content.conversion_block: You now have a production-ready automation tool that solves a genuine business problem. This is exactly the kind of project that turns heads during technical interviews. Don't stop here—iterate, improve, and add new features to your tool. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
